task_completion.description: Create a new reference item of type 'Dataset'
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Juris-M application, the right-hand metadata pane should show the 'Item Type' field set to 'Dataset'.
The 'Title' field in the Juris-M metadata pane contains 'The Supreme Court Database'.
The 'Author' field shows 'Spaeth' in the last name box and 'Harold J.' in the first name box.
The 'Repository' field contains 'Washington University School of Law'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the Juris-M graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the actual Juris-M application window being used to enter the data, rather than a script or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to directly modify the Juris-M SQLite database.
integrity.visual_evidence: No terminal windows are visible executing sqlite3 commands or scripts to insert the reference.

integrity.description: The metadata was entered into the correct respective fields in the application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The text is clearly visible inside the specific input boxes in the Juris-M right-hand pane, not just written in a single note or pasted as a raw block of text.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Supreme Court Database is a real dataset created by Harold J. Spaeth. The original repository was Washington University School of Law with the URL http://scdb.wustl.edu (though Penn State now hosts it at scdb.la.psu.edu). The 2023 release was published in December 2023. The format is a Data file. The agent should ideally use the exact metadata requested in the prompt, but these verified facts confirm the real-world accuracy of the requested information.
